title: Careers | Brown Immigration Law
content:
Brown Immigration Law is seeking a Case Manager/Legal Assistant in our Columbus, Ohio office to assist attorneys in preparing legal filings for diverse clients in the dynamic field of immigration law. We are seeking an employee that is highly organized and capable of handling multiple tasks simultaneously, as well as someone interested in working with individuals from all parts of the globe. Fluency in Spanish as a second language is required. Previous experience in working with a law firm is also desired, but not required.
Passionate about making a real impact in people’s lives through immigration law? Interested in representing an incredibly diverse client base ranging from Fortune 100 companies to individuals seeking asylum protection? Ready to finally have a career that challenges you? Brown Immigration Law is looking for a passionate, creative and driven Associate Attorney to join our Orlando office and bring your dedication and enthusiasm to our team!
About Us Brown Immigration Law is a national immigration practice devoted entirely and exclusively to the practice of US immigration law. We are dedicated, driven and passionate about our practice and pride ourselves on practicing in all areas of US immigration law with our work ranging including our employment, family and litigation groups. We started with a single office over two decades ago and now have five offices nationwide, including two in Florida (Orlando and Tampa), two in Ohio (Cleveland and Columbus) and one in North Carolina (Raleigh-Durham). Our attorneys are diverse, experienced and extremely collaborative between offices, which makes us a unique firm that prides itself on finding solutions and achieving results for our clients. Our Orlando office is situated near Lake Eola and features a multilingual staff that is interested in creativity, achievement and ensuring successful outcomes for our clientele.
Your Mission Get ready to work directly with clients from all walks of life helping individuals, families, and businesses navigate their immigration journeys. You’ll play a vital role in processing visas, handling green card filings, guiding individuals through the naturalization process, and representing clients in immigration court. You will have the opportunity to work directly with employers, large and small, as well as families, and individuals. Interested in litigation? You will have the opportunity to challenge the US Department of Homeland Security and practice before the Executive Office of Immigration Review and the Board of Immigration Appeals, through oral arguments, brief writing and trials. You’ll team up with our skilled Partners, Lead Attorneys, and support staff to transform lives by helping clients gain citizenship, reunite with their families, or avoid deportation.
What We’re Looking For No prior experience in immigration law? No problem! We’re seeking smart, driven, capable go-getters who are eager to learn and grow with us. The perfect fit for this role: – Is a natural problem solver with a can-do attitude. – Is not afraid to tackle unusual fact patterns or an ever-changing legal landscape – Is passionate about advocacy. – Is not afraid of interacting with an eclectic and diverse client base. – Is able to communicate effectively and work independently in a fast-paced, diverse, and dynamic environment. – Is not just looking for a job, but is ready for a career. Is organized and ready to take on new challenges.
The Essentials – JD Degree from an accredited law school (or equivalent). – Bar Licensure allowing the practice of law in any state. – Spanish or other foreign language skills? Not a requirement, but certainly a big plus! We have a huge and diverse clientele that speak any number of languages, including Spanish, French, Portuguese and Creole, among others. The ability to speak with a client in their own language is a significant benefit, but is certainly not mandatory.
Perks – Competitive salaries with potential for annual raises and discretionary bonuses, as well as earned compensation based upon client development – 401(k) and matching options – Flexible Health and Dental insurance – Health Savings Account – Generous Paid Time Off policy – Team outings, social events, 5k runs and office parties.
Compensation We offer competitive compensation that reflects your experience and growth as an attorney and reward the unique expertise each attorney brings to our team. We also want our attorneys to grow and become leaders within our firm and encourage them to grow their individual practices, which in turn, provides avenues for increased compensation.
The Application Process Still here? If you’re into the challenge of an incredible career with an incredibly dedicated group of practitioners and staff, send a cover letter and resume to: hr@brownimmlaw.com
Why Join Brown Immigration Law? Brown Immigration Law is more than a law firm—it’s a dynamic, collaborative practice that fuels growth, champions positive change, and creates lasting impacts in the lives of individuals and families pursuing brighter futures. With five offices and a rapidly growing presence, we offer expert legal counsel and dedicated representation in all areas of immigration law. Our global practice reaches clients across the nation and around the world. Join us and become part of a team that empowers its attorneys to thrive and make a meaningful, tangible difference in people’s lives.
Ready to dive into a rewarding career in immigration law? Apply now and become an essential part of Brown Immigration Law’s mission to change lives!
